get.description:
Returns a unified, cursor-paginated activity feed combining asset and project events across the entire workspace, sorted newest-first. Useful for building an activity timeline or a notification feed.

Each event in the response includes an entity_type (asset or project) and entity_id so the caller can link back to the originating resource.

Supported query parameters:
  • limit — Number of events (1–100, default 20).
  • cursor — Opaque cursor from next_cursor in the previous response.
  • user_id — Filter to events by a specific user.
  • types — Comma-separated event type filter.

Exports up to 10,000 workspace audit events (both asset and project) as a text/csv download. The response sets Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="activity-export.csv".

CSV columns: event_id, event_type, entity_type, entity_id, actor_type, actor_name, payload_summary, created_at.

Supported query parameters:
  • since — Exclude events before this date (inclusive, YYYY-MM-DD).
  • until — Exclude events after this date (inclusive, YYYY-MM-DD).
  • format — Currently only csv is supported.

400.description: Invalid date format or unsupported format

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of assets. The behaviour is controlled by query parameters:
  • q — Full-text search across filenames and custom field text values.
  • tags — Comma-separated tag names. Returns only assets that have ALL listed tags (AND logic).
  • folder_id — Filter by folder. Use root to list assets with no folder in a project.
  • project_id — Filter by project (required when folder_id=root).
  • mime — Filter by MIME type prefix (e.g. image/ or video/mp4).
  • sort — Sort order: created_at_desc (default), created_at_asc, size_asc, size_desc, id_asc, id_desc, taken_at, taken_at_desc.
  • limit — Page size, 1–100 (default 50).
  • cursor — Opaque cursor from the previous page's next_cursor field.
  • field[key] — Filter by custom field value, e.g. field[status]=published.

parameters.description: Folder ID (use 'root' for unfoldered assets in a project)
parameters.name: folder_id

post.description:
Uploads a file as a new asset in the workspace. The request must be a multipart form with a file field. Optional form fields:
  • project_id — assign the asset to a project on creation.
  • folder_id — assign the asset to a folder on creation.
On success a thumbnail generation job is enqueued automatically. An asset_created audit event is written and custom fields are inherited from the project if applicable.

delete.description: Permanently deletes all listed assets, their storage files, thumbnails, variants, and associated data. Assets not found in the workspace are silently skipped. This action cannot be undone.

patch.description:
Applies the same set of field value changes to multiple assets in a single transaction. Assets not belonging to the workspace are silently skipped. Returns the count of assets successfully updated.

Supports the same value format and null-for-clear semantics as the single-asset PATCH endpoint.

post.description: Assigns all listed assets to the given project. Set project_id to null to remove assets from their current project. Assets not found in the workspace are silently skipped.
post.summary: Bulk assign assets to a project

parameters.description: Asset IDs and optional project ID
parameters.name: body

post.description: Adds a single tag to all specified assets. If the tag does not exist in the workspace it is created automatically. Assets not found in the workspace are silently skipped.

get.description: Returns the full asset record including tags, version count, and a flag indicating whether variants are currently being rebuilt.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the asset record, its storage file, and its thumbnail. All associated variants, versions, tags, and field values are also removed via cascade. This action cannot be undone.

patch.description: Updates the asset's folder_id and/or project_id. Set either field to null to remove the assignment. The target folder and project must belong to the same workspace.

get.description: Returns all public share comments that have been posted on this asset across all shares.

Returns paginated audit events for the given asset. Events record every significant lifecycle action — uploads, renames, tagging, sharing, version changes, and more.

Supported query parameters:
  • limit — Number of events to return (1–200, default 50).
  • cursor — Opaque cursor from the previous page's next_cursor field.
  • types — Comma-separated list of event types to filter to (e.g. asset_shared,asset_renamed).

Returns all custom field values set on the asset, including fields for which no value has been set yet (those have value: null). Each entry includes the field definition metadata (key, name, field_type) alongside the typed value. The definition_deleted flag indicates the field definition was soft-deleted — the value is still stored but the field is no longer active.

patch.description:
Sets or clears one or more custom field values on the asset. Each entry in the values array targets a field by field_id. To clear a value, pass null as the value. All values are validated before any writes.

Value types must match the field definition's field_type:
  • text / url — string
  • number — number
  • date — string in YYYY-MM-DD format
  • boolean — boolean
  • select — string matching one of the field's defined options
Returns the full updated field values for the asset.

get.description: Streams the raw file bytes of the asset's current version. The response Content-Type matches the asset's MIME type and Content-Disposition is set to inline with the original filename. An asset_downloaded audit event is recorded (browser image prefetch requests are excluded).

Applies a resize/format transform to the asset's current version in memory and returns the result directly (never stored). Responses are cached in an LRU cache (100 entries, 5-minute TTL) so repeated identical calls are cheap.
Query parameters:
  • w — Target width in pixels
  • h — Target height in pixels
  • fit — Fit mode: contain (default), cover, fill
  • format — Output format: jpeg (default), png, webp
  • q — JPEG quality 1–100 (default: encoder default)
Only supported for image assets. Returns 400 for video, audio, or PDF assets.

put.description: Updates the asset's original_filename. The new name must include the correct file extension matching the asset's MIME type.

get.description: Returns a flat array of tag name strings applied to the specified asset.

post.description: Adds a tag to the specified asset. If the tag does not exist in the workspace it is created automatically. Also updates the tag's last_used_at timestamp.

delete.description: Removes the association between the tag and the asset. The tag itself is not deleted from the workspace. Tag name matching is case-insensitive.

get.description: Streams the JPEG thumbnail for the asset. Thumbnails are generated asynchronously after upload; returns 404 if generation has not yet completed.

404.description: Asset not found or thumbnail not ready

get.description: Returns all generated variants for the asset's current version, plus a rebuilding flag that is true when a variant rebuild job is in progress (e.g. after a new version upload). Each variant includes a download_url for direct file access.

post.description:
Enqueues a background job to generate a transformed variant of the asset's current version. Supported types and their required params:
  • image_resize — {"width": N, "height": N, "fit": "contain|cover|fill"}
  • image_convert — {"format": "jpeg|png|webp|avif"}
  • image_crop — {"x": N, "y": N, "width": N, "height": N}
  • image_watermark — {"text": "...", "position": "..."}
  • image_smart_crop — {"width": N, "height": N} (AI-assisted)
  • image_bg_remove — requires REMOVEBG_API_KEY env var
  • video_transcode — {"format": "mp4", "codec": "h264"}
  • video_capture_image — {"time_sec": N}
Returns a job ID immediately; poll GET /api/v1/assets/:id/variants to check completion. Returns 409 if a variant rebuild is already in progress.

400.description: Invalid variant type or wrong asset type

post.description: Accepts a multipart file upload and stores it as a variant of type manual on the asset's current version. Unlike transform-generated variants, manual variants are not automatically rebuilt when a new version is uploaded — they persist across version changes. Use this to attach pre-processed or third-party exports (e.g. color-corrected TIFF, print-ready PDF).

parameters.description: File to attach as a manual variant
parameters.name: file

delete.description: Permanently removes the variant record and its stored file. Only variants attached to the current version can be deleted manually — variants on older versions are removed automatically by the retention purge job.

422.description: Variant belongs to a previous version

get.description: Streams the variant's stored file. Content-Type is derived from the file extension. An asset_variant_downloaded audit event is recorded (browser image prefetch excluded).

404.description: Asset, variant, or file not found

get.description: Returns all versions of an asset in reverse chronological order. Each version includes its version_num, MIME type, dimensions, file size, optional comment, and the number of variants derived from it (variant_count). The current version has is_current: true.

post.description:
Uploads a new file as the next version of an existing asset. The new version is immediately promoted to current. The previous current version is retained in history.

After upload, the server:
  • Enqueues a thumbnail generation job for the new version.
  • Enqueues a variant rebuild job that recreates all variants from the previous current version.
  • Clears the asset's cached thumbnail (pending the new job).
If the uploaded file is byte-for-byte identical to the current version (same content hash), the request is rejected with 409 to prevent no-op versions.

The response body contains both the new version object and the updated asset object.

409.description: File is identical to the current version

delete.description:
Soft-deletes an asset version. The current version cannot be deleted — restore a different version first, then delete the old one. A version also cannot be deleted while it is in use as a project cover or workspace icon (returns 409).

Soft deletion marks the row as deleted; the storage file is not immediately removed (physical cleanup is handled by a background retention job).

409.description: Version is in use as a cover/icon

get.description: Streams the raw file for the specified version. The response MIME type and Content-Disposition are set from the stored version metadata.

404.description: Asset, version, or file not found

post.description:
Promotes a non-current version to be the current version of the asset. The asset's thumbnail is also updated to match the restored version. An audit event records the version numbers rolled back from and to.

Returns 409 if the requested version is already current. Returns 422 if the version has been soft-deleted. The response contains the updated version and asset.

get.description: Returns the JPEG thumbnail for the given version. Returns 404 if the thumbnail has not yet been generated (the job runs asynchronously after upload — poll until the thumbnail_url field in the version response is non-null).

404.description: Asset, version, or thumbnail not ready

get.description:
Opens a persistent Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream scoped to the authenticated user's workspace. The connection stays open until the client disconnects. A keep-alive comment is sent every 25 seconds to prevent proxy timeouts.

Each event is delivered as a standard SSE data: line containing a JSON object with at minimum an event field. Current event types:
  • thumbnail_ready — emitted after an image or video thumbnail job completes; payload contains asset_id.
Clients should reconnect automatically on disconnect using the browser's native EventSource API or equivalent.

Returns all custom field definitions for the workspace filtered by scope. Field definitions describe the schema for structured metadata that can be attached to assets (scope=asset) or projects (scope=project).

Supported field types: text, number, date, boolean, select, url.

post.description:
Creates a new custom field definition for assets or projects. The key must be lowercase alphanumeric with underscores and is immutable after creation. A maximum of 50 definitions per scope is enforced.

For select fields, supply an options array of string choices. The inherit_from_project flag (asset-scope only) causes the field to automatically pre-populate from the assigned project's value.

409.description: Key already exists in this scope

put.description: Updates the position of multiple field definitions in a single request. The request body is a JSON array of {"id": "...", "position": N} objects. Items not belonging to this workspace are silently skipped (best-effort update).

get.description: Returns a single custom field definition.

put.description:
Updates a custom field definition. The key and field_type are immutable after creation — supplying a different value returns 422. All other fields are optional; omitted fields retain their current values.

For select fields, options must be a non-empty JSON array of strings.

400.description: Invalid options format or empty name

422.description: Attempt to change immutable key or field_type

delete.description: Soft-deletes the field definition. The definition is hidden from the list endpoint but existing field values on assets and projects are preserved. This action is permanent from the API perspective (no undelete endpoint).

get.description: Returns the number of assets and projects that have a value set for this field definition. Useful for understanding the impact of deleting a field.

put.description: Updates the folder's name and/or position. All fields are optional. Renaming automatically regenerates the folder's URL slug.

409.description: A folder with that name already exists here

delete.description: Permanently deletes the folder and any child folders. Assets inside the deleted folder(s) are not deleted — their folder_id is set to null so they remain in the project.

get.description: Returns the most recent 50 ingress log entries across all sources in the workspace. Filter by status using the status query parameter to focus on entries that need attention (e.g. error or skipped).

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, fetching, done, error, skipped)
parameters.name: status

delete.description: Permanently removes a log entry. Because ingress deduplication is based on (source_id, remote_id), deleting an entry allows the same remote file to be re-ingested on the next poll. Use this to force a re-import of a previously processed file.

403.description: Entry belongs to a different workspace

post.description:
Resets the log entry status to pending and enqueues a new ingest_fetch job to re-attempt asset import. Only entries with status error or skipped can be retried; attempting to retry a done or pending entry returns 400.

Returns the queued job ID. The retry runs asynchronously — poll the log entry to track completion.

400.description: Entry is not in error or skipped state

403.description: Entry belongs to a different workspace

get.description: Returns all ingress sources in the workspace. Sensitive config fields are redacted with "***" in every response.

post.description:
Creates a new ingress source for automated asset import. The config object is source-type-specific (see type documentation). Sensitive fields (passwords, keys, tokens) are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM and redacted ("***") in all API responses.

Supported source types:
  • imap — Poll an IMAP mailbox for attachments.
  • sftp — Poll a remote SFTP directory.
  • webdav — Poll a WebDAV endpoint.
  • s3 — Poll an S3-compatible bucket.
  • email_api — Receive assets via SMTP push (uses public_token).
Optional rules array in the body bootstraps ingress rules for the source in a single request. Rules can also be managed later via the /rules sub-resource.

get.description: Returns the source record including its current enabled state and decrypted (but redacted) config. Sensitive config values are always replaced with "***".

put.description:
Updates the source label, config, destination folder/project, enabled state, and poll interval. Omitted fields keep their current values.

To update the config, supply a full replacement object — partial config merging is not supported. Sensitive fields provided in the new config are encrypted and stored; omitting them removes them. To clear dest_folder_id or dest_project_id pass an explicit null value.

delete.description: Permanently removes the source and all its associated rules. Log entries are retained for auditing. This action is irreversible.

get.description:
Returns the most recent 50 ingress log entries for the given source. Each entry represents one remote item that the source discovered, with its current ingestion status (pending, fetching, done, error, skipped).

The (source_id, remote_id) pair is unique — re-encountering the same remote file on a subsequent poll is a no-op unless the log entry is deleted or retried.

post.description:
Immediately enqueues an ingest_poll job for the source, regardless of its scheduled poll interval. Use this to force a check for new assets without waiting for the next scheduled run. Returns the queued job ID for tracking.

The job runs asynchronously; 202 Accepted means the job was enqueued, not that the poll completed. Monitor the ingress log to see results.

Validates that the source can successfully connect to the remote system using its current configuration. The check runs with a 10-second timeout. Returns {"ok": true} on success, or a descriptive error message on failure.

Use this after creating or updating a source to confirm credentials and network access are correct before relying on scheduled polling.

422.description: Connection test failed — body contains the error message

get.description: Returns all projects in the workspace, each with an asset_count field reflecting the number of assets currently assigned to it.

post.description: Creates a new project container. Projects group assets and folders together. An optional color (hex) and description can be provided at creation time.

get.description: Returns the project with the given ID. Returns 404 if the project does not exist or belongs to a different workspace.

put.description: Updates the project's name, description, color, or cover asset. All fields are optional — only the fields present in the request body are updated; omitted fields retain their current values.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the project. Assets that belonged to the project are not deleted — their project_id is set to null so they remain accessible in the unfiltered asset library.

get.description: Returns paginated audit events for the given project. Supports the same limit, cursor, and types query parameters as the asset events endpoint.
Returns all custom field values set on the project. Only fields with scope=project are included. The response structure mirrors the asset fields endpoint.

patch.description:
Sets or clears one or more custom field values on the project. Only fields with scope=project are accepted — submitting an asset-scoped field returns 422.

Pass null as the value to clear a field. Returns the full updated field values for the project.

422.description: Value validation failed or wrong scope

get.description: Returns a recursive tree of all folders in the project (up to 2 levels deep). Each folder includes its children in a children array and an asset_count showing how many assets are directly inside it (not counting descendants).

post.description:
Creates a new folder in the given project. Folders are at most 2 levels deep — a root folder can have child folders, but child folders cannot have their own children.
Use parent_id to create a sub-folder inside an existing root folder. An optional position integer controls ordering in the UI.

400.description: Parent folder belongs to a different project

404.description: Project or parent folder not found

409.description: A folder with that name already exists here

get.description: Returns all shares created in the workspace, including revoked and expired ones. Use the revoked_at and is_expired fields to filter active shares client-side.

post.description:
Creates a public share link for the given target (asset or project). Options:
  • password — Protect the share with a password.
  • expires_in_days — Automatically expire the share after N days.
  • allow_download — Whether viewers can download original files (default true).
  • allow_comments — Whether viewers can post comments (default false).
The response includes a public_url that can be shared directly with external reviewers.

404.description: Target asset or project not found

get.description: Returns the share record including its current status (active, revoked, expired).

put.description: Updates the share's label, password, expiry, and permission flags. All fields are optional. To remove an existing password send "clear_password": true. To remove an expiry send "clear_expiry": true.

delete.description: Sets revoked_at on the share so that all future public access attempts return 404. The share record is retained for audit purposes. Revocation is permanent.

get.description: Returns all comments posted through the share, visible to workspace members for moderation. The authenticated user must be a member of the workspace that owns the share.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a comment posted on the share. Only workspace members can use this endpoint. Use it to moderate inappropriate or spam comments.

get.description: Returns all tags in the workspace, each with asset_count, optional color, group_name, and last_used_at.

post.description: Creates a new tag with an optional color (hex) and group name. Tag names are case-insensitive and must be unique within the workspace.

delete.description: Deletes all listed tags and removes their associations from all assets. Tags not found in the workspace are silently skipped. Returns the count of tags deleted and the total number of asset-tag associations removed.

post.description: Reassigns all asset-tag associations from the listed source tags to the target tag, then deletes the source tags. The target tag is created if it does not already exist. Useful for consolidating duplicate or near-duplicate tags.

parameters.description: Source tag names and target tag name
parameters.name: body

200.description: merged_assets count and updated target TagResponse

get.description: Returns pairs of tags whose names are similar (Levenshtein distance < 30% of the longer name) among tags that have at least one associated asset. Up to 20 pairs are returned, sorted by similarity score ascending (most similar first). Use the merge endpoint to consolidate them.

patch.description: Updates one or more attributes of an existing tag identified by its name. All fields are optional. Renaming checks for conflicts with existing tag names.

post.description: Creates a new workspace and makes the authenticated user its owner. The user may belong to multiple workspaces; use POST /api/v1/workspace/switch to activate a different workspace in subsequent requests.

get.description: Returns all workspace invites that have not yet been accepted and have not expired. The invite_token field is omitted from list responses for security — it is only returned once, when the invite is created.

post.description: Generates a time-limited invite token (7-day expiry) for the given email address. The invite_token in the response should be sent to the recipient; they redeem it via POST /auth/invite/accept. Only workspace owners may create invites.

delete.description: Cancels a pending invite, preventing the recipient from accepting it. Has no effect on already-accepted invites (they are already marked as used).

post.description:
Enqueues a background job for the entire workspace. Currently supported job types:
  • extract_exif — Backfills EXIF metadata (make, model, GPS, taken-at, etc.) for all image assets that do not yet have the _exif_make custom field populated.
Returns the number of assets enqueued for processing.

get.description: Returns the workspace embedded in the auth token, the authenticated user's profile, and their membership role in that workspace. Use this endpoint on app startup to hydrate the session.
Returns all members of the workspace embedded in the auth token, including their names, emails, roles, and join dates.

put.description: Changes the role (owner, editor, or viewer) of a workspace member. Safeguard: the last owner cannot demote themselves.

parameters.description: User ID whose role to change
parameters.name: userId

delete.description:
Removes a user from the active workspace. Safeguards:
  • A user cannot remove themselves.
  • The last owner of a workspace cannot be removed.

400.description: Cannot remove yourself or the last owner

put.description:
Updates the workspace's version retention policy and EXIF metadata settings.
  • version_retention_count: Maximum number of asset versions to keep per asset. 0 = keep all.
  • exif_keep: Whether EXIF metadata is preserved on uploaded images.
  • exif_keep_gps: Whether GPS coordinates are preserved within EXIF data (requires exif_keep=true).

post.description: Issues a fresh JWT scoped to the requested workspace and updates the auth_token cookie accordingly. All subsequent authenticated requests will operate in the new workspace context. The user must be a member of the target workspace.

403.description: Not a member of this workspace

get.description: Returns every workspace the authenticated user is a member of, along with their role in each. Use this to populate a workspace-switcher UI.

post.description: Redeems a one-time invite token: creates a new user account (name + password required) and adds them to the workspace at the role encoded in the invite. The invite token is marked as accepted and cannot be reused. Returns a JWT just like POST /auth/register.

Authenticates the user with email and password. Returns a JWT token in the response body and sets an auth_token httpOnly cookie (7-day expiry). The workspace embedded in the response is the user's first workspace — use POST /api/v1/workspace/switch to change it.
Clears the auth_token httpOnly cookie by setting it to an expired value. No server-side session state is maintained, so this is a client-side-only operation.

Reissues a fresh JWT with a new 7-day expiry for the authenticated user and workspace. The new token is returned in the response body and also set as an updated auth_token cookie. Call this endpoint before the current token expires to maintain a session without re-login.

Creates a new user account with name, email, and password. A default workspace is automatically created for the new user. The response includes a JWT auth token set as an auth_token httpOnly cookie and also returned in the response body.

get.description: Returns public feature flags and configuration values that the frontend needs before authentication. Currently exposes the demo boolean which, when true, puts the server into read-only demonstration mode.
get the status of server.

Returns the share's label and whether it requires a password. Use this as the first call when a visitor lands on a share link — if has_password is true, show a password gate before calling POST /shared/:id/access. Returns 404 if the share does not exist or has been revoked, and 410 if it has expired.

post.description: Validates the share (checks password if one is set) and issues a 24-hour share session token. Include this token as Authorization: Bearer <token> in all subsequent requests to /shared/:id/* endpoints. Also increments the share's view count.

get.description: Returns all assets accessible through the share, along with share metadata (allow_comments, allow_download, etc.). The asset set depends on the share's target type: a single asset for asset shares, all project assets for project shares. Requires a valid share session token.

410.description: Share has expired or been revoked

get.description: Returns a single asset accessible via the share. Returns 404 if the asset is not part of the share's target. Requires a valid share session token.

404.description: Asset not found in this share

get.description: Returns all comments posted on a specific asset within the share.

404.description: Asset not found in this share

get.description: Streams the original file for an asset accessible via the share. Returns 403 if the share was created with allow_download: false.

403.description: Download not allowed for this share

get.description: Streams the JPEG thumbnail for an asset accessible via the share. Thumbnails are always accessible regardless of the allow_download setting, as they are needed for review purposes. Returns 404 if the thumbnail has not yet been generated.

404.description: Asset not found or thumbnail not ready

get.description: Returns all comments on the share, grouped by asset_id. Useful for rendering a review dashboard showing all feedback at once.

post.description: Posts a comment on an asset within the share. The share must have allow_comments: true. Commenters identify themselves by name; an optional email can be provided. Returns 403 if comments are disabled for this share.

403.description: Comments not allowed for this share

404.description: Asset not found in this share

expires_at.description: ISO string or null to clear
